title: Rework Ast hashing to be stable
body:
Note: this depends on #2967, which is the first of the 2 patches, so ignore that. If you prefer we can combine these 2 PRs, but this second one is more likely to cause issues, so might be good to keep them separate I'm happy to rework #2967 based on feedback. This PR is marked as Draft only beacuse of this dependency, otherwise it's good to go.

Regarding the hashing, it is all stable now, working towards #2958. 

The commit message says:
```
Eliminated reliance on pointer values in AstNode hashes in order to make
them stable. This requires moving the sameHash functions into a visitor,
as nodes pointed to via members (and not child nodes) need to be hashed
themselves.

The hashes are now stable (as far as I could test them), and the impact
on verilation time is small enough not to be reliably measurable.
 ```

Regarding performance. The test suite (`time make test`), with 100% ccache hit rate, before this change:
```
make test  4190.18s user 1716.78s system 1492% cpu 6:35.84 total
```
With this change:
```
make test  4151.96s user 1707.60s system 1483% cpu 6:35.00 total
```
Output on my large design is identical, and `perf` shows nothing reliably measurable in the profile related to the hashing, so that's all good as far as I can see.

You will notice I removed `V3DupFinder::sameHash`. This was only used in V3Gate, but this caused a problem, as V3Gate computes hashes of AstActive nodes via this `sameHash` function which contain the assignments V3Gate is modifying, but computing the hash of the AstActive now requires computing the hash of its contents, and V3Hasher then caches the hash of the assignments as well. A deduped signal on the RHS of such assignments is then replaced, which would have to invalidate the cached hash, otherwise it misses transitive dedup opportunities (which t_dedupe_clk_gate finds). It's a lot simpler just not to use `V3DupFinser::sameHash`, as it's only supposed to be a speedier `AstNode::sameTree`, but now hashing itself requires a traversal in most cases, and `AstNode::sameTree` can discriminate non-same trees quickly, it only needs to do a full traversal for   trees that are indeed identical, in which case the hashes should have been identical as well, so they wouldn't have helped. Also profiling shows this has no performance impact.

We could optimize a bit further by setting the `hashDtype` and `hashChildren` flags to false in more cases, but the hashing is already not performance critical.

Ext tests are passing.
